title: Israel, Lebanon reach framework deal; US mediator says agreement is 'first step' toward peace, releases text
cleaned_news: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio announced on Friday (local time) that the United States had brokered a framework agreement between Israel and Lebanon designed to promote “lasting peace and security”. Under the agreement, a ceasefire would take effect if Hezbollah halts all attacks and withdraws from southern Lebanon. The agreement states that this process depends on “the verified disarmament of non-state armed groups and dismantlement of associated infrastructure, enabling the [Israeli army] to progressively redeploy out of the Lebanese territory”. Rubio described the agreement as the first and most difficult step toward that goal, acknowledging that much work remained while stressing its importance.
bullets: • US Secretary of State Marco Rubio announced a framework agreement between Israel and Lebanon.
• The agreement was designed to promote “lasting peace and security” between the two nations.
• A ceasefire would take effect if Hezbollah halts all attacks and withdraws from southern Lebanon.
• The Lebanese military will restore effective sovereign authority over all Lebanese territory under the agreement.
• The process depends on the verified disarmament of non-state armed groups and dismantlement of associated infrastructure.

vocabularies.meaning_en: Arranged or negotiated (a deal or agreement).

error_spotting.sentence: The US State Department released the text of the agreement that was finalised earlier in Washington, DC, reporting Al Jazeera.
error_spotting.rule: The sentence requires a past participle 'reported' to indicate that Al Jazeera was the source that reported the information, acting as an adverbial phrase modifying the main clause. 'Reporting' would imply Al Jazeera is actively reporting at the moment of the statement, which is not the intended meaning in this context.

fill_in_the_blanks.sentence: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio announced on Friday (local time) that the United States had _____ a framework agreement between Israel and Lebanon designed to promote “lasting peace and security”.
